dimensions.name: ac-coverage
dimensions.evidence: All 11 ACs and AC-Guard verified. AC-1: schema has optional supersedes property, all 43 rows validate. AC-2: definition.yaml has {commit: e4adef5, row: pi-step-ceremony-and-artifact-enforcement}. AC-3: parseArgs registers 'supersedes-confirmed' as a value flag (row.go:401). AC-4: rowBlockers emits supersedence_evidence_missing when DefinitionSupersedes != nil and flag is absent or mismatched. AC-5/AC-6: test scenarios A, B, C cover negative paths with appropriate blocker messages. AC-7: scenario E confirms no blocker when values match. AC-8: state.json.archived_at=2026-04-25T04:01:09Z, phase_a.notes='supersedence confirmed: e4adef5:pi-step-ceremony-and-artifact-enforcement', phase_a.blockers=[]. AC-9: 'Recommended next slice' section is absent from handoff.md (grep-c returns 0). AC-10: 'furrow row list --active' does not include pi-adapter-foundation. AC-11: .focused contains 'pi-row-audit-cleanup', not pi-adapter-foundation. AC-Guard: scenario F confirms no supersedence_evidence_missing blocker when DefinitionSupersedes is nil.

dimensions.name: schema-additivity
dimensions.evidence: supersedes is present in schemas/definition.schema.json properties block (lines 107-123) but NOT in the top-level 'required' array (which lists only: objective, deliverables, context_pointers, constraints, gate_policy). All 43 existing .furrow/rows/*/definition.yaml files validated via 'frw validate-definition' — all passed. Schema shape matches spec exactly: type=object, required=[commit,row], additionalProperties=false, with pattern on row field.

dimensions.name: rowblockers-integrity
dimensions.evidence: All 4 call sites updated correctly: row.go:201 (runRowTransition) passes rowBlockersOpts{} zero value; row.go:439 (runRowArchive) passes archiveOpts with SupersedesConfirmed and DefinitionSupersedes populated; row.go:610 (buildRowStatusData) passes rowBlockersOpts{}; review.go:58 (runReviewStatus) passes rowBlockersOpts{}. Struct is minimal — two fields only, zero-value safe as specified. Guard condition correctly gates on opts.DefinitionSupersedes != nil before any check (row_workflow.go:1026). definitionSupersedes() helper is clean, uses existing yaml.Unmarshal pattern, returns nil on any error or absent key.

dimensions.name: test-rigor
dimensions.evidence: row_workflow_test.go has 5 table-driven scenarios: A (missing flag, expects blocker with e4adef5 and pi-step-ceremony-and-artifact-enforcement in message), B (mismatched commit badc0de, expects blocker with both e4adef5 and badc0de), C (mismatched row wrong-row-name, expects blocker with both required and actual row names), E (matching flag, expects NO supersedence blocker), F (nil DefinitionSupersedes, expects NO supersedence blocker). Assertions check both blocker presence/code and wantMsgContains substrings. One minor gap: Scenario D (both commit and row mismatched, 'single blocker not two' assertion) from the spec is not implemented as a standalone case — it would be covered by B/C logic but the count=1 guarantee is not explicitly tested. This is a minor omission, not a blocking deficiency.

dimensions.name: live-archive-evidence
dimensions.evidence: state.json.archived_at='2026-04-25T04:01:09Z' (non-null). gates/review-to-archive.json exists with phase_a.blockers=[] (empty array confirmed via Python parse). phase_a.notes='supersedence confirmed: e4adef5:pi-step-ceremony-and-artifact-enforcement' (exact string match). review->archive gate entry appears in state.json.gates array with outcome=pass and evidence_path pointing to the gate file. Pre-archive transitions are recorded: implement->review gate evidence='no implement work — phantom row, scope satisfied by sibling row pi-step-ceremony-and-artifact-enforcement (commit e4adef5)'. The full 7-gate sequence is present in state.json (ideate->research through review->archive).

dimensions.name: phantom-review-artifacts
dimensions.evidence: Three review JSON files exist in reviews/: row-model-continuation.json, evaluator-grade-review-semantics.json, archive-disposition-signals.json — one per deliverable in definition.yaml. All three are schema-valid (phase_a.artifacts_present, phase_a.acceptance_criteria array, phase_b.dimensions, overall verdict, corrections, reviewer, timestamp fields present). All three are required artifacts for the review step because reviewArtifactsForRow() derives required artifacts from rowDeliverableNames(), which reads from definition.yaml — so the harness correctly demanded 3 review artifacts. The workaround is justified: these phantom reviews encode the supersedence rationale per-deliverable, giving audit readers a clear chain (criterion -> met:true -> evidence pointing to commit e4adef5) rather than a vague global note. This is NOT a leaky abstraction — the review-step artifact enforcement is working exactly as designed; the phantom row had deliverables in its definition.yaml, so review artifacts were required. The only smell is that row-model-continuation.json has a 'review_phase_a_evidence_thin' warning (1 criterion with thin evidence), but this is a warning not a blocker and the overall verdict is still 'pass'. The artifacts are properly hand-authored with supersedence-aware evidence strings, not auto-generated nonsense.
dimensions.notes: The 'warn' status on row-model-continuation is visible in the gate evidence (archive_ceremony.review.by_status shows pass=2, warn=1) but did not block archive because artifact_validation produces 0 blocking_count. This is correct harness behavior.

dimensions.name: constraint-compliance
dimensions.evidence: No direct edits to state.json — state mutations went through Go CLI (writeJSONMapAtomic inside runRowArchive, which is CLI-mediated). state.schema.json was not modified (only definition.schema.json was changed). No shell mutations to state — rws was not used for the archive path. bin/rws untouched in wave 3 (no wave 3 changes to bin/rws visible; spec §Wave 3 Shared File Edits confirms bin/rws requires no changes for wave 3). The implement->review gate went through rws transition (CLI-mediated). Gate evidence is written via writeGateEvidence() (CLI-mediated).

dimensions.name: wave-isolation
dimensions.evidence: app.go runRow switch contains both 'case "repair-deliverables"' at line 96 (wave 1) and 'case "archive"' at line 94 (pre-existing, used by wave 3). The wave 3 changes to runRowArchive's parseArgs are in row.go, not app.go — app.go only needed the routing case which was already present. No wave 1 additions were clobbered. Both repair-deliverables and archive cases coexist in the switch statement as confirmed by grep.

notes:
The implementation is technically sound across all 8 dimensions. The rowBlockersOpts pattern is idiomatic Go and correctly zero-value safe. Schema additivity is confirmed by bulk validation of all 43 rows. The live archive evidence is complete and correctly structured.

The phantom-review-artifacts dimension is the most interesting. Three hand-authored review JSON files exist for a row that did no implementation work — this is not a smell but a feature of how the harness works: definition.yaml lists 3 deliverables, so reviewArtifactsForRow() requires 3 review artifacts, and the harness correctly enforced this. The author wrote supersedence-aware evidence strings that make the audit trail readable (each criterion's 'evidence' field traces back to commit e4adef5). The single 'warn' on row-model-continuation (1 criterion with thin evidence) is visible and does not block archive — this is the expected behavior for a phantom row where one criterion's evidence is inherently indirect. The workaround is justified and documented through the gate evidence notes field. There is no leaky abstraction: the review-step artifact enforcement fired correctly on a row that had deliverables in its definition.yaml; it would only be problematic if a row with zero deliverables had been forced to produce review artifacts, which is not the case here.

One minor gap: Scenario D (asserting exactly 1 blocker when both commit and row are wrong) is described in the spec but not implemented as a named test case in row_workflow_test.go. The logic would produce exactly 1 blocker (the mismatch case is a single switch branch), but this is not explicitly asserted. This is a low-severity documentation gap, not a behavioral defect, and does not warrant a dimension downgrade.
